engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking out a physical hostel — deciding by photos, room types, amenities, map/location and what's nearby. Browsing the place IS the decision; the site only marks the argument and hands booking off elsewhere.

goal.one_action: click Book now / START YOUR TRIP to reserve a bed/room (or make a group inquiry)

structure.archetype_note: Multi-property place site: home hub → per-location property pages (rooms/amenities/map/FAQ) → thin book-now destination-picker that redirects per location to external Cloudbeds engine; separate group-inquiry form path.

mapped.observed_as: Our hostels / Pariwana Cusco / Pariwana Lima
Live It Up at Pariwana! / Free Features / Additional Features / Enjoy Pariwana
Check Our Great Location / What's Nearby / How to Get There
Travel FAQs / Quick Answer / Frequently asked questions
Select a destination / Have questions before booking?

unmapped.observed: What's Nearby (nearby attractions list per property)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A local-attractions/things-to-do list is a place-specific content type with no canonical section; part travel-guide, part local SEO — not features/useCases/map cleanly.

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Every Book now / START YOUR TRIP CTA hands off per-location to the external Cloudbeds reservation engine (hotels.cloudbeds.com/reservation/...); the thin on-site book-now page is a 716-char destination picker with no on-site inventory or checkout.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full; all 5 core pages, CTAs, forms and embeds captured. Cloudbeds handoff and thin book-now page confirmed via both static and rendered fetch.

gap_findings: Multi-property (per-location) place site: home hub linking to N property subpages each with own rooms/map/FAQ — closest existing capability is practitioner-profiles but that's per-person; a per-location/property variant is unmodeled.
'What's Nearby' local-attractions list has no canonical section (falls to miscellaneous).
Trilingual (en/es/pt) exceeds the bilingual capability's two-language framing.
Room-type listing without on-site prices (rates only behind Cloudbeds) sits awkwardly between catalog and pricing sections.
